code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Predicate-to-phrase mapping with natural phrasing for astronaut domain
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "is",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "flew on",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "included",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Extract all subjects and objects to detect relationships
    subjects = set(t.subject for t in triples)
    objects = set(t.object.strip('"') for t in triples)

    # Group triples by subject
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Build main subject's information
    main_subject = list(grouped.keys())[0]
    main_triples = grouped[main_subject]

    # Build clauses for main subject
    main_clauses = []
    related_subjects = {}

    for t in main_triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
        obj = t.object.strip('"')

        # Special handling for specific predicates
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
        elif t.predicate == "awards":
            if obj.isdigit():
                obj = f"{obj} awards"
        elif t.predicate == "status":
            obj = obj.lower()
        elif t.predicate == "mission":
            obj = f"the {obj} mission"
        elif t.predicate == "birthPlace" or t.predicate == "deathPlace":
            obj = f"{obj}"

        main_clauses.append(f"{verb} {obj}")

        # Check if object is another subject (for relative clauses)
        if obj in subjects:
            related_subjects[obj] = grouped.get(obj, [])

    # Build main sentence
    if len(main_clauses) == 1:
        main_sentence = f"{main_subject} {main_clauses[0]}"
    elif len(main_clauses) == 2:
        main_sentence = f"{main_subject} {main_clauses[0]} and {main_clauses[1]}"
    else:
        main_sentence = f"{main_subject} " + ", ".join(main_clauses[:-1]) + f", and {main_clauses[-1]}"

    # Add related information with relative clauses
    if related_subjects:
        for related_subj, rel_triples in related_subjects.items():
            rel_clauses = []
            for t in rel_triples:
                verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                if t.predicate == "country":
                    rel_clauses.append(f"is in {obj}")
                elif t.predicate == "capital":
                    rel_clauses.append(f"where the capital is {obj}")
                else:
                    rel_clauses.append(f"{verb} {obj}")

            if rel_clauses:
                rel_text = ", ".join(rel_clauses)
                main_sentence += f" which {rel_text}"

    return main_sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
